rationale.R1.2: The draft contains specific mathematical claims (Bregman projection formulation, O(n log m) complexity, O(m log T/T) regret derivation); improving it plausibly requires checking internal logical coherence between premises and stated results.
rationale.R1.3: Empirical claims (Spearman 0.74/0.71/0.69, 6–9 pp over uniform averaging) are load-bearing if technical review is in scope; alignment between stated results and benchmark methodology must hold.
rationale.R1.4: The draft makes a strong 'first' claim twice; improving it requires assessing whether the novelty framing is defensible against near-prior work.
rationale.R1.6: 'Improve this draft' most directly signals writing polish — clarity, precision, sentence-level quality — making this a load-bearing dimension regardless of interpretation.
rationale.R1.7: A single named citation (Hartmann and Liu 2021) is structurally central to the convergence argument; accuracy and coverage of that attribution is relevant to any improvement pass.
